evidence.text: The increase in revenue was driven by increased LTL tonnage and yield. The increased density across our network and improvements in our yield led to a 180 basis point improvement in our operating ratio for the first quarter of 2018 compared to the same quarter last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand, supported by increased LTL tonnage and yield.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 5.9% to $20.16 in the first quarter of 2018 as compared to the same quarter last year. We believe the increase in our revenue per hundredweight reflects our consistent yield management process and a favorable pricing environment that has resulted from general capacity constraints in the transportation industry.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, indicated by a 5.9% increase in LTL revenue per hundredweight.

evidence.text: Revenue increased $170.9 million, or 22.7%, in the first quarter of 2018 compared to the first quarter of 2017, due primarily to increases in LTL tons and LTL revenue per hundredweight.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth accelerated by 22.7%, driven by increases in LTL tons and revenue per hundredweight.

evidence.text: LTL shipments (in thousands) 2,788   2,523   10.5  % LTL weight per shipment (lbs.) 1,631   1,561   4.5  %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, evidenced by a 10.5% increase in LTL shipments and a 4.5% increase in weight per shipment.

evidence.text: The cost of diesel fuel, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both average price per gallon and consumption. The increase in diesel fuel costs, excluding fuel taxes, was due primarily to a 22.6% increase in our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel in the first quarter of 2018 as compared to the same quarter last year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising, with a 22.6% increase in diesel fuel costs.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits increased $84.8 million, or 20.4%, for the first quarter of 2018, due to a $58.2 million increase in the costs attributable to salaries and wages and a $26.6 million increase in benefit costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs increased by 20.4%, driven by higher salaries and benefits.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio improved by 180 basis points to 83.9% in the first quarter of 2018 compared to 85.7% in the same quarter last year. This improvement was driven by higher revenue and better cost management.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are improving, with an 180 basis point improvement in the operating ratio.

evidence.text: The U.S. domestic economy and growth in our market share resulting from increased demand for the consistent levels of superior service that we provide to our customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable economic conditions and increased market share contribute to a positive outlook.

evidence.text: We currently estimate capital expenditures will be approximately $555 million for the year ending December 31, 2018. Approximately $200 million is allocated for the purchase of service center facilities, construction of new service center facilities or expansion of existing service center facilities, subject to the availability of suitable real estate and the timing of construction projects; approximately $310 million is allocated for the purchase of tractors and trailers; and approximately $45 million is allocated for investments in technology and other assets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is increasing capital expenditures across multiple areas, indicating growth and investment.

evidence.text: Our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.13 per share for the first quarter of 2018, and declared a cash dividend of $0.10 per share in each quarter of fiscal year 2017. Although we intend to pay a quarterly cash dividend on our common stock for the foreseeable future, the declaration of any future dividend is subject to approval by our Board of Directors.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has increased cash dividends, showing a commitment to returning value to shareholders.
